title: crotalaria afrocentralis
intro: Crotalaria afrocentralis, also known as the Central African rattlebox, is a plant species in the legume family Fabaceae that is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a purgative and a diuretic, and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Crotalaria afrocentralis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and treat skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and seeds are typically dried and then used to make teas or infusions, or the seeds are crushed to make a powder that can be used as a medicinal supplement.
